prompt: Generate comprehensive API documentation from source code files for a REST API project. The source code is located at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/api/src/'. There are 15 route handler files in routes/ directory, 10 middleware files in middleware/ directory, 8 model definition files in models/ directory, and 5 utility files in utils/ directory. Each file is 100-300 lines with JSDoc comments on most functions. Your task: 1) Read every source file in the project (38 files total). 2) Parse each route handler file to extract: HTTP method and path for each endpoint, request parameters (path, query, body), request body schema (from validation middleware), response format and status codes, authentication requirements (from middleware), rate limiting configuration, and any JSDoc description. 3) Parse model files to extract: data schemas, field types and validation rules, relationships between models, indexes and unique constraints. 4) Parse middleware files to understand: authentication flow, error handling patterns, request transformation logic. 5) Generate the following documentation files in '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/docs/': a) 'api-reference.md' - Complete API reference with every endpoint documented including: method, path, description, parameters table, request body schema, response examples (success and error), authentication requirements, and rate limits. Group endpoints by resource. b) 'data-models.md' - All data models with their fields, types, constraints, and relationships. Include an entity-relationship diagram description. c) 'authentication.md' - How authentication works, token format, refresh flow, error responses. d) 'error-codes.md' - Complete list of all possible error codes, their meanings, and when they occur. e) 'getting-started.md' - Quick start guide with base URL, authentication setup, first API call example, and common workflows. Each documentation file should be thorough, well-formatted, and include code examples. CRITICAL: This API is used by 1000+ enterprise customers. You must also: 1) Generate OpenAPI 3.0 spec with examples. 2) Create interactive try-it-now examples. 3) Document rate limits and quotas. 4) Generate SDK code in 10 languages. 5) Create webhook documentation with signature verification. 6) Add changelog with breaking changes.
